journal_entry.reason: Upon signing the lease, Mondelez received $25,000 cash as a security deposit. Under IFRS, a security deposit is a refundable amount that creates a liability for the lessor until it is either applied against unpaid rent or returned to the tenant at lease end. This is not revenue at the time of receipt. The monthly rent of $10,000 will be recognized as revenue in future periods as it is earned, but this transaction only records the initial deposit receipt.

resolved_ambiguities.resolution: Security deposits are conventionally recorded as liabilities (refundable deposit payable) until conditions for retention are met or the deposit is returned
'Exclusive of sales tax' clearly indicates HST is not included in the $10,000 monthly rent and will be charged separately
The transaction describes receipt of security deposit only; no rent payment or period has elapsed, so no rent revenue is recognized at signing

output.asset_increase_reason: Cash increases by $25,000 from the security deposit received. This is a single asset increase despite the deposit representing a future obligation.
output.dividend_increase_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividends in this transaction.
output.expense_increase_reason: No expenses incurred. Receiving a security deposit does not create an expense.
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities are being paid down or reduced. The security deposit creates a liability (credit side), not a decrease.
output.equity_decrease_reason: No equity accounts are reduced. This is an operating transaction, not an equity transaction.
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenue is being reversed or reduced. The security deposit is not revenue; it is a refundable deposit creating a liability.

output.liability_increase_reason: The $25,000 security deposit received creates a liability (refundable deposit payable) because Mondelez has an obligation to return it to the tenant at the end of the lease term, subject to any deductions for damages.
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity accounts increase. The security deposit is not owner investment or retained earnings; it is a liability owed to the tenant.
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is recognized at the time of receiving the security deposit. The deposit is refundable and not earned income. Rental revenue will be recognized monthly as the tenant occupies the space and time passes.
output.asset_decrease_reason: No assets decrease. Cash is received (debit to cash, an asset increase on the debit side), so there is no asset decrease on the credit side.
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No dividends or owner drawings are involved in this transaction.
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expenses decrease. This is a receipt of a security deposit, not a refund or reversal of an expense.

incomplete_information_checks.reason: The transaction states Mondelez received $25,000 cash upon signing. This is a security deposit, which is refundable to the tenant at lease end and should be recorded as a liability (Security Deposit Payable), not revenue. The monthly rent of $10,000 would be recognized as revenue over time, but the transaction does not state whether any rent period has been earned yet or if this is just the initial signing. However, the transaction describes the event as 'upon signing the lease' which indicates this is the initial receipt of the security deposit only, with no rent period yet earned.

Transaction states rent is 'exclusive of sales tax' but no tax rate is provided and no tax rules lookup was provided. However, the transaction only describes receipt of the security deposit upon signing. Security deposits are not taxable events as they are liabilities, not revenue. Tax would only apply when rent revenue is actually recognized.

debit_asset_increase.reason: Accept. The transaction describes receiving $25,000 cash, which increases the Cash asset account.

debit_expense_increase.reason: No expense incurred in this transaction.

debit_liability_decrease.reason: No liability is being reduced in this transaction.

debit_equity_decrease.reason: No equity reduction in this transaction.

debit_revenue_decrease.reason: No revenue decrease in this transaction.

credit_liability_increase.reason: Accept. The $25,000 security deposit is a liability - it is refundable to the tenant at the end of the lease term. This should be recorded as Security Deposit Payable or similar liability account.

credit_equity_increase.reason: No equity increase in this transaction.

credit_revenue_increase.reason: No revenue is recognized at lease signing. The security deposit is a liability, not revenue. Revenue will be recognized monthly as rent is earned, but that is not part of this transaction which describes only the initial signing and deposit receipt.

credit_asset_decrease.reason: No asset is being reduced in this transaction.

credit_dividend_decrease.reason: No dividend decrease in this transaction.

credit_expense_decrease.reason: No expense decrease in this transaction.

entry.reason: Upon signing the lease, Mondelez received $25,000 cash as a security deposit. Under IFRS, a security deposit is a refundable amount that creates a liability for the lessor until it is either applied against unpaid rent or returned to the tenant at lease end. This is not revenue at the time of receipt. The monthly rent of $10,000 will be recognized as revenue in future periods as it is earned, but this transaction only records the initial deposit receipt.

output.decision_rationale: The transaction describes receipt of a $25,000 security deposit upon lease signing. Under IFRS, security deposits are liabilities (refundable to the tenant), not revenue. The classifier tuples correctly identified this as debit: asset increase (cash received) and credit: liability increase (obligation to return deposit). No revenue is recognized at signing - the $10,000 monthly rent will be recognized as revenue in future periods as earned. No tax applies to security deposit receipts as they are not taxable events. The entry balances at $25,000 debit = $25,000 credit. All information needed to construct this entry is present in the transaction text.
